# loom (full context)

loom is built for review-comment workflows in GitHub pull requests.

Primary use cases:

1. Enumerate unresolved review threads
2. Filter by path/author/severity/text
3. Reply with status/resolution comments
4. Resolve/unresolve threads
5. Re-check unresolved count until zero

## Commands

### List threads

`loom list --pr <number> [flags]`

Flags:

- `--repo owner/repo` (optional, inferred from upstream git remote)
- `--state unresolved|resolved|all` (default: unresolved)
- `--path <substring>`
- `--author <login>`
- `--contains <text>`
- `--severity critical|major|minor`
- `--sort updated|created|path|line|author|severity` (default: updated)
- `--desc` (default: true)
- `--limit <n>`
- `--json`
- `--stats`

### Find comments by query

`loom find --pr <number> --query <text> [flags]`

`find` accepts all main filter/sort/output flags from `list`.

### Reply

`loom reply --pr <number> --comment <database-id> (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--json]`

If `--body` and `--body-file` are omitted, stdin is used.

### Resolve

`loom resolve --thread <PRRT_...> [--json]`

### Unresolve

`loom unresolve --thread <PRRT_...> [--json]`

## Output conventions

- Human mode: tabular output for `list/find`; short status lines for action commands.
- JSON mode:
  - `list/find`: array of thread records
  - `reply`: object with action/comment_id/reply_id/url
  - `resolve/unresolve`: object with action/thread_id

## Operational notes

- Requires authenticated `gh` session.
- Uses GitHub GraphQL for thread data and resolve mutations.
- Uses GitHub REST endpoint for review comment replies.
